snippet.title: Stephen King on Writing: Don't Use A Notebook
snippet.description:
A tip from one of the most prolific writers in our industry. While romanticised and immortalised in the writer's experience, the notebook might be your worst ally when coming up with ideas. 

One counter thought to this: when writing a big novel, creating an outline or mapping your characters isn't a bad idea. It also heavily depends on whether you're a "gardener" or an "architect" - a subject we've covered at length.

snippet.title: CRAZED Black Bear Fatally Mauled Jacqueline On Her Anniversary Trip
snippet.description:
It was September, 2005. At that point, Mark Jordan and Jacqueline Perry had been married for almost four years, and their anniversary was coming up. They were both very active people who liked outdoor activities, so finding the perfect spot to spend their anniversary wasn’t probably too hard for the two of them. 

They lived in Cambridge, a city in the Regional Municipality of Waterloo, Ontario, Canada; so, they were well accustomed to modern life and its demands. In fact, both Mark and Jacqueline had important jobs. He was a software engineer, and she was a medical doctor. Despite the challenging character of their respective jobs or maybe because of it, the young couple tried not to ignore their innate inclination toward nature. That’s why, besides his regular job as a software engineer, Mark was also a Scout leader.

In fact, both Mark and Jacqueline had important jobs. He was a software engineer, and she was a medical doctor. Despite the challenging character of their respective jobs or maybe because of it, the young couple tried not to ignore their innate inclination toward nature. snippet.title: Human Volition | Patrick Haggard (2016)
snippet.description:
"VOLITION, AGENCY, RESPONSIBILITY:
COGNITIVE MECHANISMS OF HUMAN ACTION"
Lecture Series by Patrick Haggard

Mardi 17 mai 2016 de 14h30 à 16h30. 
École normale supérieure, 29, rue d'Ulm 75005 Paris. Salle Jean Jaurès

Lecture 1: Human Volition

Voluntary action is central to our views of human nature, but eludes scientific investigation. Philosophers sometimes define voluntary actions as actions which are reasons-responsive, or are “up to us”.  In contrast, neuroscientists often consider voluntary actions as involving a specific set of brain pathways that lead to movement. In particular, brain science distinguishes between movements that are externally-triggered, such as reflex responses, and those that are internally-generated.This lecture considers what this capacity for internally-generated movement might mean, what basis it might have in the human brain, and how it relates to our conscious experience of our own actions.

The complete lecture series:
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLplZlMKmYMNEpkXmMLm_cUcEgty3FrxJe

snippet.title: 9th Global Annual Conference of the ILO Global Business and Disability Network, 28 Nov 2022
snippet.description:
For the ninth time, the ILO Global Business and Disability Network (GBDN) held its global annual conference – this time in a virtual format. The programme included the welcoming of ILO GBDN members that joined in 2022, the presentation and discussion of corporate practices by Multinational Enterprises on topics like digital skilling and employment, disability-inclusive procurement as well as the inclusion of neurodivergent talent. Further, new guidance on disability Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) in global companies and the updated ILO GBDN Self-Assessment Tool for companies were launched at the conference.

International Sign interpretation and live captioning were provided.

00:00:00 Opening remarks and summary of work in 2022
00:04:00 Alex Lane, Disability & Neurodiversity Inclusion Lead, Accenture - Chair of the ILO Global Business and Disability Network
00:13:53 Neil Milliken, Global Head of Accessibility, Atos - Vice-Chair of the ILO Global Business and Disability Network
00:16:00 Chidi King, Chief of Gender, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Branch, International Labour Organization
00:21:27 Jürgen Menze, Disability Inclusion Officer, International Labour Organization
00:31:17 Welcoming of and statements by new members

00:42:27 Robert Oosting, Head of Diversity & Inclusion, ISS 
00:46:48 Béatrice Chavanel, VP Diversity & Inclusion, Renault Group
00:53:30 Julie Feniger, Project Manager, Inclusion Manifesto (Manifeste Inclusion) France
00:58:00 Skilling and employment in the digital economy
00:58:15 Christine Hamot, Disability Manager, TotalEnergies, and Laurent Dordain, Diversity & Inclusion Manager, ENGIE

localized.title: 9th Global Annual Conference of the ILO Global Business and Disability Network, 28 Nov 2022
localized.description:
For the ninth time, the ILO Global Business and Disability Network (GBDN) held its global annual conference – this time in a virtual format. The programme included the welcoming of ILO GBDN members that joined in 2022, the presentation and discussion of corporate practices by Multinational Enterprises on topics like digital skilling and employment, disability-inclusive procurement as well as the inclusion of neurodivergent talent. Further, new guidance on disability Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) in global companies and the updated ILO GBDN Self-Assessment Tool for companies were launched at the conference.

International Sign interpretation and live captioning were provided.

snippet.title: Responsible gaming impeded by social stigma: Dr Anastasia Hronis
snippet.description:
Experts have recently argued the importance of messaging to take the social stigma out of Responsible Gaming. In this week’s Face-to-Face, we speak to Dr. Anastasia Hronis, clinical psychologist and founder of the Australian Institute for Human wellness and an expert in mental health and addiction. We discuss where the stigma comes from, particularly amongst Asian cultures, and how operators can better deal with this on the gaming floor. 

Chapters: 
00:00 Intro
00:59 Gambling stigma  
03:07 Approaching gamblers 
07:00 Self-exclusion  
09:17 RG messaging

snippet.title: Orange Business Summit 2022 : l’évènement du business à impact positif
snippet.description:
Découvrez la keynote d'ouverture de l'Orange Business Summit 2022 et les plénières.
Orange Business Summit était de retour le 27 septembre 2022 autour de la thématique « Créons ensemble un impact positif pour vos clients, pour vos équipes et pour la société ».
Une 4ème édition riche en contenus, innovations, expériences et échanges. 

Au programme :

 "Le B2B au cœur de la stratégie d’Orange" avec  Christel Heydemann, Directrice Générale Orange.

 "La digitalisation au service d’une croissance responsable" avec
Aliette Mousnier-Lompré, Directrice Générale Orange Business Services, 
Bruno Bailly, Directeur Général Merem, et Jean-Paul Mazoyer, Directeur Général adjoint, Groupe Crédit Agricole.

 "Construire ensemble un environnement de confiance" avec
Nassima Auvray, Directrice de la stratégie et du marketing des solutions Cloud de confiance, Orange Business Services et Patrick Fasso, Directeur Technique et des systèmes d’information, Radio France.

"Orange Business Services engagé pour une innovation à impact positif"
Wassila Zitoune-Dumontet, Chief Operating Officer Orange Business Services et Sébastien Picardat, Directeur Général Agdatahub, interrogent le rôle de l’innovation au service d’un développement vertueux, inclusif et éthique.

snippet.title: A Warning To All Crypto Currency Holders
snippet.description:

snippet.title: Inflation Perspiration | LSE Executive Education
snippet.description:
Brunello Rosa’s research examines the rise in inflation in recent years and how long the current wave being seen by the global economy is likely to last. Using consumer and investment behaviours and modelling it against previous economic crises, this research looks at the implications of fiscal stimulus and how governments and central banks should respond to this current wave of inflation. 

LSE’s five-day intensive course ‘Global Macroeconomic Challenges’ utilises the latest theory and research on challenges being faced by the global economy today and will empower you with the knowledge and insights to make sense of the road that lies ahead.

For more information please see: https://www.lse.ac.uk/study-at-lse/executive-education/programmes/global-macroeconomic-challenges

snippet.title: Global Recession Coming in 2023 (IT'S GOING TO BE BAD...)
snippet.description:
A new study from the World Bank suggests that a massive global recession is coming in 2023 and that the Fed’s policies are only making this crash more likely. 

Though it has technically been just two years since the last global downturn—the 2020 episode brought on by the pandemic—these kinds of worldwide recessions are relatively rare. 

In the last 50 years, only five of them have occurred: in 1975, 1982, 1991, 2009, and 2020.

Today’s conditions bear striking similarities with stagflation in the 1970s, which accompanied an extended period of high inflation and subdued growth worldwide. 

That episode spurred the kind of monetary policy responses that led to the 1982 global recession and marked the start of decades-long debt crises in many developed economies. 

Today, the world economy is already experiencing much sharper slowdowns in growth than was seen during the lead-up to previous global downturns, so what does this mean for us in the coming months and years?
